sections.paragraph:
μικρολογία, ας, ἡ (‘a small matter’ Pla. et al.) someth. of little or no account, trifle in a disdainful sense (Pla., Hipp. Maj. 304b of hair-splitting in debate; Lucian, Vit. Auct. 17; Philo, Somn. 1, 94) Hm 5, 2, 2.—DELG s.v. μικρός.

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

someth. someth. = something

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Hm Hm = Mandates

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 650
